description_long: This factory building complex consists of three main brick structures with flat, concrete roofs and multiple large, industrial-style windows on the front facades. The buildings are arranged side by side, with the central two featuring prominent loading dock doors at ground level, suitable for freight or vehicle access. Scattered around the buildings are various industrial elements, including utility poles, antennas, and concrete barriers, enhancing the utilitarian, industrial atmosphere. The brickwork is uniform and weathered, with visible mortar lines and a slightly rough texture, while the roofs are plain and functional. No smokestacks are present, but the presence of rooftop equipment and external fixtures suggests active industrial use. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on function over form, characteristic of early-to-mid 20th-century industrial architecture.
description: A group of three brick factory buildings with flat roofs, large industrial windows, and loading dock doors, surrounded by various industrial fixtures. The design is utilitarian and functional, with visible rooftop equipment and weathered brickwork.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the buildings and the arrangement of external industrial fixtures along the side.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the main facades with large industrial windows and two prominent loading dock doors at ground level.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the rear and side walls, with minimal window openings and visible rooftop equipment.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain brick backs of the buildings and the flat, unadorned rooftops.
